title: #[non_exhaustive] on all spec-defined enums guards against silent breakage from future spec additions
description: ChatKind, DeliveryState, ContactPresence, ChatMemberRole, ChannelPermissionTargetType, OwnerPresence, and SseEvent are all marked #[non_exhaustive]. A future contributor will want to remove this for ergonomic match patterns (no _ arm required). That would be wrong: the spec is a draft (draft-atwood-jmap-chat-00). When the spec adds a new presence state or delivery state, downstream code that exhaustively matches will silently stop compiling after the library adds the new variant — forcing callers to handle it. With #[non_exhaustive], existing code keeps working (the _ arm already exists) and the library can add variants in a semver-compatible way. This is especially important for SseEvent, where unrecognized event types must fall through silently.
close_reason: Decision record — current design is correct, no change needed
